name: Vicksburg Railroad
description_short: Federal credit union in Vicksburg, MS serving 1,000+ members with $12M in assets. Offers checking, savings, loans, and payday alternative loans.
description_long:
Vicksburg Railroad Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union headquartered in Vicksburg, Mississippi, with deep community roots dating back to 1934—92 years of continuous operation. As a federally chartered institution regulated by the NCUA, it serves approximately 1,004 members through a single branch location and a small but dedicated staff of 5 employees.

The credit union offers a range of standard financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and multiple loan products. Their loan portfolio is diverse, encompassing real estate loans ($620K), unsecured personal loans ($587K), and other loan categories totaling $7.48M in outstanding loans. Notably, they offer Payday Alternative Loans (PALs), which provide members with small-dollar credit under 36% APR as an alternative to predatory payday lending.

Vicksburg Railroad FCU is distinguished by its hyperlocal focus and long institutional history in the Vicksburg community. With $12M in total assets and $8.1M in member deposits, it maintains a conservative balance sheet with strong capital reserves ($3.89M in equity). The organization operates with modest overhead—$388K in total annual operating expenses and compensation—allowing it to maintain accessibility for its membership base.

This is a small, traditionally-operated credit union best suited for members seeking personal banking relationships in a local institution rather than competitive rates or cutting-edge digital features. The organization is financially stable but constrained by its small asset base, which may limit product innovation or competitive lending rates compared to larger credit unions. Members should expect basic but reliable service with emphasis on community connection.
services: Money Market and Other Deposit Products
NCUA Share Insurance (up to $250K per member)
pros: 92-year history demonstrates institutional stability and deep community roots since 1934
Offers Payday Alternative Loans (PALs) capped under 36% APR for members needing emergency cash
Strong capital position with $3.89M in equity reserves relative to $12M asset base
NCUA-insured member deposits provide federal protection up to $250K per account
Low operating expense ratio ($388K annual operating costs) suggests efficient management
Membership-based governance means members have voting rights in organizational decisions
Accessible local branch at 2101 Cherry St in Vicksburg with direct phone support (601-638-3134)
cons: Only $12M in total assets limits product diversity and competitive loan rates versus larger credit unions
Single branch location provides no geographic flexibility for members outside Vicksburg
Small membership base (1,004 members) may limit cross-selling opportunities and scale benefits
No evidence of digital banking platform or mobile app services in website content
Zero investment portfolio suggests limited wealth management or investment services beyond basic banking
best_for: Vicksburg-area residents seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making
Members needing payday alternative loans under 36% APR without predatory terms
Consumers prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and member-owned governance structure
Individuals with strong community ties seeking to support local financial institution
diagnosis: Vicksburg Railroad FCU is best for existing Vicksburg-area residents or railroad-affiliated individuals seeking basic banking and emergency small-dollar loans through a locally-controlled, federally-insured institution with 92 years of stability. The primary caveat is limited scope—single location, small asset base, and basic service menu make it unsuitable for members requiring competitive rates, digital banking, investment services, or geographic flexibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
